svg.text: Seeing purity in oneself, one becomes more at ease in forest dwelling
AUSPICIOUS NIGHTS OF THE LUNAR FORTNIGHT
Dwell in terrifying and hair-raising places as park shrines, forest shrines, and tree shrines
FACING FRIGHT AND DREAD IN FOUR POSTURES
When fright and dread arises · does not stand still or sit or lie down
but remains walking and subdues it there
When fright and dread arises · does not start walking or sit or lie down
but remains standing and subdues it there
When fright and dread arises · does not lie down or stand or start walking
but remains sitting and subdues it there
When fright and dread arises · does not sit up or stand or start walking
but remains lying down and subdues it there
Perceives night as night, day as day — not subject to delusion
rattiṁyeva samānaṁ rattīti sañjānāmi, divāyeva samānaṁ divāti sañjānāmi · asammohadhammo
Born of seclusion (vivekaja) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukha)
Free from reflection and examination (avitakka · avicāra) · Born of collectedness (samādhija) · Imbued with uplifting joy and pleasure (pītisukha)
With the fading of desire for uplifting joy (pītiyā ca virāgā) · Equanimous (upekkhaka) · Mindful (sato)
Clearly aware (sampajāno) · Experiencing pleasure with the body (sukha kāyena)
Abandoning bodily pleasure and pain (sukhassa ca pahānā dukkhassa ca pahānā) · Settling of mental pleasure
and displeasure (somanassadomanassānaṁ atthaṅgamā) · Purification of mindfulness through equanimity
The Divine Eye: Seeing According to Kamma
Ignorance is dispelled, true knowledge arises; darkness is dispelled, clarity arises
avijjā vihaññati vijjā uppajjati · tamo vihaññati āloko uppajjati
The Blessed One resorts to secluded lodgings in remote forests and woodlands
Pleasant abiding in the here and now
Out of compassion for future generations
